title: Kit (a stamped family capability that binds kernel contracts)
description: A kit is a stateless plugin that binds the kernel's contracts to one family (corpus, app, …): the family ontology, the rubric manifest (which validated rubric cells gate which layers), the validation harness adapters, the dispatch policy, and seed patterns. The kernel defines contracts; a kit implements them. The boundary's falsification test (the kit-conform gate): adding a new kit must require ZERO kernel edits — a kit that needs the kernel changed has leaked. A kit holds no instance state.

family.description: The family this kit serves (corpus, app, …); binds its dispatch policy and adapters.

kernel_compat.description: The dev-kernel version range this kit binds (semver range). kit-conform rejects an incompatible bind.

ontology.description: Cell ids of the family ontology cells this kit seeds (the controlled vocabulary for the family).

rubric_manifest.description: Which validated rubric cell gates which layer/scope of this family's work. A ticket's acceptance.rubric_cell must resolve here.

rubric_cell.description: Cell id of the bound rubric (must reach `validated` before it gates anything).

adapters.description: Validation harness adapters: how this family runs a verifier to mint a signal for a layer/scope. Each must conform to adapter.schema.json (kind=validation); kit-conform checks them.

dispatch_policy.description: Path (kit-relative) to this family's dispatch-policy.json (DispatchPolicy) — the deterministic unit→execution-plan map.

seed_patterns.description: Seed pattern artifacts (or cell ids) the kit ships to warm the family's pattern layer and the compass cold-start priors.
